text: Nūn. By the pen and what they inscribe,
You are not, [O Muḥammad], by the favor of your Lord, a madman.
And indeed, for you is a reward uninterrupted.
And indeed, you are of a great moral character.
So you will see and they will see
Which of you is the afflicted [by a devil].
Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.
tl: Innaa Rabbaka Huwa a'lamu biman dalla 'an sabeelihee Wa-Huwa a'lamu bilmuhtadeen

text: Then do not obey the deniers.
They wish that you would soften [in your position], so they would soften [toward you].
And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer
[And] scorner, going about with malicious gossip -
A preventer of good, transgressing and sinful,
Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.
Because he is a possessor of wealth and children,
When Our verses are recited to him, he says, \"Legends of the former peoples.\"
tl: Izaa tutlaa 'alaihi aayaatunaa qaala asaateerul awwaleen

text: We will brand him upon the snout.
Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning
tl: Innaa balawnaahum kamaa balawnaaa As-haabal jannati iz 'aqsamoo la-yasri-munnahaa musbiheen

text: Without making exception.
So there came upon it [i.e., the garden] an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep.
tl: Fataafa 'alaihaa taaa'i-fum mir rabbika Wa-hum naaa'imoon

text: And it became as though reaped.
And they called one another at morning,
[Saying], \"Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit.\"
tl: Anigh doo 'alaa harsikum in kuntum saarimeen

text: So they set out, while lowering their voices,
[Saying], \"There will surely not enter it today upon you [any] poor person.\"
And they went early in determination, [assuming themselves] able.
But when they saw it, they said, \"Indeed, we are lost;
Rather, we have been deprived.\"
The most moderate of them said, \"Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allāh]?'\"
tl: Qaala awsatuhum alam aqul lakum law-laa tusabbihoon

text: They said, \"Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers.\"
tl: Qaaloo subhaana rabbinaaa innaa kunnaa zaalimeen

text: Then they approached one another, blaming each other.
They said, \"O woe to us; indeed we were transgressors.
Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous.\"
tl: Asaa rabbunaaa any yubdilanaa khairam minhaaa innaaa ilaa rabbinaa raaghiboon

text: Such is the punishment [of this world]. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew.
tl: Kazaalikal azaab, Wa-la'azaabul aakhirati akbar; law kaanoo ya'lamoon

text: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure.
Then will We treat the Muslims like the criminals?
What is [the matter] with you? How do you judge?
Or do you have a scripture in which you learn
That indeed for you is whatever you choose?
Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge?
tl: Am lakum aymaanun 'alainaa baalighatun ilaa yawmil qiyaamati inna lakum lamaa tahkumoon

text: Ask them which of them, for that [claim], is responsible.
Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful.
tl: Am lahum shurakaaa'u fal-ya'too bishurakaaa-'ihim in kaanoo saadiqeen

text: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but they [i.e., the disbelievers] will not be able,
tl: Yawma yukshafu 'am saaqinw Wa-yud'awna ilas sujoodi falaa yastatee'oon

text: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.
tl: Khaashi'atan absaaruhum tarhaquhum zillatunw Wa-qad kaanoo yud'awna ilas sujoodi Wa-hum saalimoon

text: So leave Me, [O Muḥammad], with [the matter of] whoever denies this statement [i.e., the Qur’ān]. We will progressively lead them [to punishment] from where they do not know.
tl: Fazarnee Wa-many yukazzibu bihaazal hadeesi sanastad-rijuhum min haisu laa ya'lamoon

text: And I will give them time. Indeed, My plan is firm.
Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?
tl: Am tas'aluhum ajran fahum min maghramin musqaloon

text: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?
Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muḥammad], and be not like the companion of the fish [i.e., Jonah] when he called out while he was distressed.
tl: Fasbir lihukmi rabbika Wa-laa takun kasaahibil hoot; iz naadaa Wa-huwa makzoom

text: If not that a favor [i.e., mercy] from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.
tl: Law-laaa an tadaara-kahoo ni'matum mir rabbihee lanubiza bil'araaa'i Wa-huwa mazmoom

text: And his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous.
And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes [i.e., looks] when they hear the message, and they say, \"Indeed, he is mad.\"
tl: Wa-iny yakaadul lazeena kafaroo la-yuzliqoonaka biabsaarihim lammaa sami'uz Zikra Wa-yaqooloona innahoo lamajnoon

text: But it is not except a reminder to the worlds.
